Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(canvas): powerful navigation — keyboard pan/zoom + mini-map

- SwiftUI @FocusState + .onKeyPress replaces broken NSView keyboard approach
  (NSView never became first responder due to SwiftUI layers intercepting clicks)
- Arrow keys pan 50 pt; Shift+Arrow pans 200 pt (fast sweep across large canvas)
- = / + zooms in ×1.25 centred on viewport; - zooms out ×0.8
- 0 or f fits all blocks to window
- Canvas auto-grabs keyboard focus on appear, tap, and drag start

- ProcessCanvasMiniMap overlay (bottom-left, 180×112 pt):
  - Shows every block as a scaled thumbnail
  - Cyan rectangle tracks the current viewport in real-time
  - Tap or drag on mini-map to instantly navigate the main canvas
  - Visibility: hidden when canvas is empty

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove redundant sleeps after blocking Travel/Undock/Dock calls

Travel, Undock, and Dock already block until the server responds.
The hardcoded sleeps (20s, 12s, 15s, 11s) after them were wasteful.
Mine also blocks via waitForActionResponse so the 11s sleep in the
mining inner loop was redundant too.

feat: Phase 7 — RFC 6350/2426 vCard implementation

- vcard.ToV4 / ToV3: 3.0↔4.0 conversion (VERSION, EMAIL types, PHOTO)
- vcard.ValidatePhotoSize: reject inline photos >250 KB (413)
- vcard.ExtractVersion: expose VERSION property
- ContactPut: convert 3.0→4.0 on ingest, enforce photo size limit
- ContactGet: content negotiation via Accept: text/vcard; version=X.Y
- PropBuilder.AddSupportedAddressData: C:supported-address-data property
- buildAddressBookProps: expose supported-address-data in PROPFIND

29/29 davlint tests pass (rfc2426 + rfc4918 + rfc6350 + rfc6764).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix filter enabled states wiped when saving general directory settings

The General & Card Display form doesn't contain filter checkboxes, so
saving it would set all filter enabled states to false. Guard filter
processing with isset(dir_filter_label) — only runs when a per-row
filter form is submitted. Also pass through directory_page_id in
per-row filter forms so it isn't lost on filter saves.

Refactoriser CSS en mobile-first (min-width)

Base styles = mobile (≤600px). Overrides progressifs :

- @media (min-width: 900px) — desktop

Sections refactorisées :
- Topbar : padding, logo height, complete-label, storage-label
- Tabs row : grid-template-columns, gap, padding
- List scroll : padding
- Item-sub : masqué mobile, visible desktop+
- Story/radiant : column mobile → row desktop
- Acts grid : 2col mobile → 3col desktop
- Daedric : 2col mobile → 4col desktop
- Shouts : 1col mobile → 3col tablette+, polices adaptées
- Enchanting : 2col mobile → 4col tablette → 6col desktop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix VM destroy leaving stale "destroying" records in orchestrator DB

The destroy handler returned early on node errors (404, unreachable) without
cleaning up the DB record. This left VMs stuck in "destroying" status forever.

Two fixes:
- client.go: Treat 404 from node as success (VM already gone)
- handlers.go: Always delete DB record after destroy attempt, regardless of
  node response. The health monitor's SyncNodeVMs catches real orphans.

commit.message:
ci: restore LEPT_TIFF_RESULT for Tesseract try_run, add BUILD_TESTS=OFF

Tesseract's CheckFunctions.cmake probes Leptonica TIFF support via
try_run() regardless of whether TIFF is enabled. All our builds use a
toolchain file, so CMake treats them as cross-compiling and try_run()
fails. Unconditionally pass LEPT_TIFF_RESULT=0 (Leptonica has no TIFF).

Also add BUILD_TESTS=OFF to avoid building Tesseract test binaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add AI Agent identity with ID-JAG token flow

Implements dual authentication architecture:
- Inbound: User authentication via Okta SSO (ID token + access token)
- Outbound: Agent authentication via private_key_jwt (RS256)
- ID-JAG: Agent exchanges user ID token for token to act on behalf of user

New features:
- Agent configuration with private key JWT (client_id: wlp2o86e2kkTN0tuS0h8)
- Client assertion generation for private_key_jwt auth
- ID-JAG token generation (token exchange or local fallback)
- Token viewer shows all three token types with flow diagram
- Agent info in health endpoint

Token Flow:
1. User → Okta SSO → ID Token + Access Token (Inbound)
2. Agent + User ID Token → Okta → ID-JAG Token (Exchange)
3. Agent + ID-JAG Token → MCP Server (Outbound on behalf of user)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix BFP8_B packing: tilize matrix before pack_as_bfp_tiles (correct GEMV output)

pack_as_bfp_tiles expects pre-tilized input where each contiguous 1024-element
block forms one 32×32 tile. We were passing raw row-major matrix data, causing
completely wrong weight tile contents and garbage model output.

Fix: reorganize [M,K] row-major bf16 data into tile-major order (each tile is
a contiguous 32×32 row-major block) before calling pack_as_bfp_tiles.

Also: switch weight reads to interleaved DRAM via TensorAccessor (replacing
incorrect DRAM-sharded bank addressing), add matmul transpose flag for correct
GEMV semantics (y = W @ x), and clean up debug prints.

Model now produces correct text: "The capital of France is Paris."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Track provider quota from warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic sessions (Forge-g5m) (#158)

* fix: track provider quota from warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic sessions

Only smith.Spawn was capturing and persisting provider quota (rate-limit
headers) to state.db. The warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic workers
all spawn claude sessions but discarded the quota data, causing the
dashboard quota display to undercount actual usage.

- Warden: call db.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- CIFix: call p.DB.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- ReviewFix: call p.DB.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- Schematic: add Quota field to Result, persist in pipeline.go

Bead: Forge-g5m

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-g5m

- cifix/reviewfix: move UpsertProviderQuota inside the provider loop so
  quota is persisted per-attempt (including rate-limited ones), fixing
  the wrong-provider bug when all providers are rate limited
- warden: same fix — upsert quota inside the loop before the early return
  on all-providers-exhausted, removing the stale "claude session" comment
- pipeline_test: add TestSchematic_Quota_PersistedToStateDB asserting that
  schematic quota is written to provider_quotas in state.db

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(hearth): rework Live Activity to use one persistent group per event type

Instead of grouping consecutive same-type events (creating many small
groups), merge ALL entries of the same type into a single persistent
group with an accumulating counter. Think groups expand by default;
all others collapse. Expansion toggling is keyed by event type string
instead of group index.

commit.message:
indexer: AST-based file walker for robust Python source extraction

Replace import-based module walking with AST file walking in
get_declarations_from_path. This avoids import-time failures from
missing dependencies, syntax issues, or incomplete environments.
Rebuilds index from 234 to 1178 entries (5x), capturing all
ageo-atoms sub-pipeline modules that previously failed to import.
